name: celestial-vault
summary: An open hall in the Upper Planes where gold, marble, and open sky blur together. Light itself is architecture — pillars of radiance support a ceiling that may not exist.

size_guidance.aspect_ratio: 2:1 with broad central axis — generous, airy, not intimate

primary_palette.note: central radiance as the sacred focus
place of judgment, weighing of souls

story_prompts: a solar presides over a judgment, the scales lit from within
empty at dawn — a single feather drifting down through a pillar of light
damaged by a fiendish raid — one pillar of light has gone out, leaving a dark scar
a planar traveler has been granted sanctuary and sleeps beneath the altar
vault is preparing for a soul's ascension — flowers laid, candles aligned

lighting_notes.ambient_intent: bright (0.6-0.75) with warm gold-white ambient color

anti_patterns: never make it dim or gloomy — celestial space is LIGHT-forward
no dark wood, no black stone — white, gold, cream, pale blue only
avoid clutter — the room's power comes from open, resonant space
don't forget that the 'ceiling' may be open sky or infinite cloud
